title: Deny External Traffic with AuthorizationPolicy
description:
## Deny External Traffic with AuthorizationPolicy

Istio `AuthorizationPolicy` provides fine-grained access control between services. A common pattern is to allow only in-mesh traffic (services with an Istio-issued identity) to reach sensitive workloads.

**Your task:**

Create an `AuthorizationPolicy` named `deny-external` in the `secure-ns` namespace that:
- **Denies all requests** that do **not** originate from within the `secure-ns` namespace
- Uses `action: DENY` with a source `notNamespaces` rule matching anything outside `secure-ns`

The policy must apply to **all workloads** in `secure-ns` (no selector).

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get authorizationpolicy deny-external -n secure-ns -o jsonpath='{.spec.action}'
```

hints.title: AuthorizationPolicy with DENY action
hints.body: Use action: DENY with a rules[].from[].source.notNamespaces field. This denies any request whose source namespace is not in the allow-list.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: deny-external
  namespace: secure-ns
spec:
  action: DENY
  rules:
  - from:
    - source:
        notNamespaces: ["secure-ns"]
EOF

validation.description: Check that the AuthorizationPolicy exists with DENY action and notNamespaces rule

commands.command: kubectl get authorizationpolicy deny-external -n secure-ns -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl get authorizationpolicy deny-external -n secure-ns -o jsonpath='{.spec.action}'
